title: The Timed Heist
logline: A crew robs a vault in exactly 9 minutes. The military precision points to a suspect who is an industrial engineer and expert in standard times.

setting.primary_location: Vault and service corridors of a currency exchange/security firm

premise.victim_profile: Robbery (no homicide); small staff held for the minimum required time
premise.inciting_event: Cameras and sensors show the vault was breached in exactly 9:00 minutes with zero improvisation

suspects.name: The Industrial Engineer
suspects.role: Expert in standard times and process design; manufacturing/lean background

suspects.name: The Head of Operations
suspects.role: Knows shifts, routes, and response times of staff and security

suspects.name: The Alarm Technician
suspects.role: Contractor with access to maintenance, sensors, and logs

suspects.name: The Ex-Military Guard
suspects.role: Trained in rapid incursions; boasts about discipline and coordination

digital_evidence_targets: Internal emails regarding time audits and drills

spatial_tool.concept: Site plan: vault, airlocks, service corridors, camera/sensor locations, and entry/exit routes

trojan_horse.concept: An apparently innocent technical report of a 'time study' includes a 9:00 standard table that matches the heist pattern and gives away the method
trojan_horse.purpose: Recontextualizes the evidence: it wasn't 'military', it was process engineering applied to crime

tone.style: Precision, timing, technical jargon vs. 'commando' narrative
